summaries.103705: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on both stable and master across Android and iOS. A fix was submitted to Skia (https://skia-review.googlesource.com/c/skia/+/541978) and subsequently merged. One commenter reported the issue was still present in Flutter 3.3 beta but then confirmed it was fixed, leaving the issue open pending stable promotion.
summaries.10713: Comments clarified the issue affects both iOS and Android (title updated accordingly). The root cause is the engine lacks a general hot-restart notification hook to reset plugin state; a pattern of sending an 'init' method via MethodChannel on hot restart was proposed as a workaround. A commenter suggested calling `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')` in `runApp()` or in `main()` as a Dart-side workaround. A team member (@loic-sharma) fixed iOS but never completed the Android fix; the issue remained open with Android unresolved and the assignee eventually removed after inactivity. The engine does expose a hot-restart notification API that embedders could use instead of a Dart-side fix.
summaries.168588: The root cause was identified in a comment: when the TextField loses focus on Firefox, Flutter removes the underlying `<input>` element from the DOM, so there is no element to receive the emoji when it is picked later. Issue confirmed reproducible on both stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0-pre on Firefox/macOS web. No fix or PR mentioned; status is open.
summaries.156950: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on master channel with iOS 18.0.1+ devices but not on iOS 15.8, establishing it as an iOS 17.5+ OS-version-specific regression. The issue was marked OS-specific. No fix or PR linked; status is stalled with no recent updates.
summaries.9343: Comments confirmed the bug still reproduces on stable 3.3 and master 3.5 with Japanese IME on iOS. A comment noted that in macOS 14 (Sonoma) the native composing style changed and is now closer to Flutter's existing rendering, narrowing the visual gap. Flutter still shows a thin black underline whereas native iOS 17 shows a thick blue underline. No fix or PR linked; stalled.
summaries.145758: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on both stable 3.19.3 and master 3.21. The root cause is that `RawAutocomplete` passes the parent context to `fieldViewBuilder`, which is outside the `Actions` widget that registers the autocomplete intents; wrapping with a `Builder` would fix it. No fix or PR linked; open.
summaries.151533: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on iPad (iPadOS 17.5.1) with stable 3.22.2 and master 3.24. The bug is iPad-specific and does not occur on iPhone. The reporter confirmed no workaround exists, characterizing it as a rendering engine or text input engine bug. Issue assigned to team-ios; no PR linked.
summaries.120763: Comments confirmed reproduction on both stable and master on web (Chrome and Safari on macOS, Windows also reported by users). The triage note identified that pressing spacebar does not commit Chinese characters but double-clicking with the mouse does. The root cause — IME getting interrupted when TextField constraints change — was not further narrowed in comments. No fix or PR linked; stalled.
summaries.95634: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on web (Chrome) but also on Windows desktop (with Alt key instead of Meta), not on macOS desktop, pointing to a framework-level key-state issue. A commenter noted a related web issue (#162977) where trigger keys are not released when the app loses focus. No fix or PR linked; stalled.
summaries.145324: Comments identified that the hit-testing problem is not specific to `PopupMenuButton` but also occurs with `TextButton` in an `InputDecoration` label. The root cause hypothesis is that `InputDecorator`'s layout of the `label` does not account for its transform during hit testing. Confirmed on stable 3.19.3 and master 3.21. No fix or PR linked; stalled.
summaries.178096: Comments show the issue is specific to physical iPads running iPadOS 26.1 (not reproducible on simulators). The floating numeric keyboard is a new iPadOS 26 behavior, also observed in native SwiftUI apps and third-party apps (Booking), suggesting an Apple-side change. A workaround was found: using `TextInputType.numberWithOptions(signed: true)` prevents the floating keyboard but adds sign characters. Verbose logs were shared for team investigation. Status: open, being investigated.
summaries.47458: Comments explain that focus notifications are deferred between frames to avoid spurious notifications when a node moves in the tree, which is why the ChangeNotifier is not called synchronously on unmount. A proposal was made to add a reason to focus change notifications (e.g., unmounted) so clients can distinguish the cause. The proposal received positive reception. No fix or PR linked; stalled.
summaries.120894: Comments confirmed the VoiceOver focus issue is web-only (works on iOS and Android). A workaround was identified: navigating outside the web page and back restores focus. A later commenter observed that typed text appears in the accessibility node but not in the visible TextField, indicating a disconnect between the accessibility tree and the rendered text field. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-web.
summaries.122721: The root cause was identified: when a `TextInputFormatter` modifies the composing region, the framework signals the Android `InputMethodManager` to restart the input connection, causing the keyboard to switch back. Confirmed on both emulator and physical devices (Gboard and Samsung keyboards). The pattern requires typing enough characters to cross a block boundary in the formatter. A workaround is `keyboardType: TextInputType.visiblePassword`. Assigned to @LongCatIsLooong but subsequently unassigned after inactivity; stalled.
summaries.153895: Comments confirm the bug reproduces with a regular `TextField`, not just the `DeltaTextInputClient` sample. Two related sequences with different expected outcomes were documented: (1) press-hold u → right → left → ESC → left arrow should remove the accented letter; (2) press-hold u → right → ESC should leave an unaccented `u`. No fix or PR linked; open with team-text-input.
summaries.20987: Comments confirm the animation lag reproduces on iOS in release mode (not in debug) and also on macOS (first navigation only). The lag is caused by the keyboard appearing during the navigation transition. Workarounds involve delaying the focus request with `Future.delayed` or using a custom keyboard. The bug also reproduces on Android. Assigned to team-framework; no PR linked; stalled.
summaries.138346: The root cause was identified as how Flutter handles the size transition when returning from an external activity (the image picker) back to the Flutter app; it is not specific to image_picker itself. Confirmed on stable 3.13.9 and master 3.17. Workarounds shared: unfocus the text field before opening the picker and refocus afterward, or close the keyboard after image selection. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-framework.
summaries.84344: Comments confirmed the issue on web and desktop: text in a TextField is not selectable by mouse/cursor/trackpad when the text exceeds the field's visible width. It was noted this may have been fixed in master 2.3.0-pre, but horizontal auto-scroll during drag selection remains unimplemented. A workaround of `scrollPhysics: NeverScrollableScrollPhysics()` prevents overflow scrolling but hides the extra text. No fix or PR for horizontal edge-scroll-during-selection; stalled with team-text-input.
summaries.180652: Comments clarify that the issue only reproduces in release mode on web when `SemanticsBinding.instance.ensureSemantics()` is called in `main()`; without that call, autofill fills all fields correctly. A team member confirmed the framework does create the form with all fields and considers this a bug. The original reporter confirmed the issue persists on master channel. Status: open, being tracked by team-web.
summaries.127181: The root cause was identified as a side effect of #119583, which made Flutter select all text on Tab focus to match web browser behavior; this also fires when `requestFocus()` is called programmatically. Confirmed web-only; mobile and desktop behave correctly. A distinction between tab-triggered and programmatic focus is not available through the focus API. Workaround: use `autofocus: true` on the `TextField` instead of `requestFocus` in `initState`. Stalled with team-web.
summaries.134268: Comments note that for composition-based IME panels (e.g., Japanese), checking `_currentEditingValue.composing.isValid` is a usable proxy for whether the panel is open. The `super_editor` team adopted this workaround. However, a follow-up report noted it breaks when the selection is at the right edge of text because the composing region is invalid even though the panel is open. No framework-level API proposed or PR linked; stalled.
summaries.86089: Comments identified the root cause: `heroes.dart` uses `manifest.navigatorSize` (captured at hero flight start) to compute `RelativeRect`, but when the keyboard dismisses during the transition the actual Navigator render box size changes, causing the hero to be sized incorrectly. Removing the bottom/right constraints or using the live render box size partially mitigates the jump but does not fully fix it. Confirmed on stable 3.19.3; stalled with team-design.
summaries.160505: Comments confirm the white area bug reproduces on a physical Android (Pixel 7) device after rotating from landscape (with keyboard open) to portrait. The bug is intermittent and was only reproduced once during triage before the keyboard auto-closed on rotation. No fix or PR linked; status open with team-web.
summaries.64059: Comments confirm horizontal edge scrolling still does not work on Android (and SelectableText), as of master on API 34. A team comment noted the bug may be Android-specific (not appearing on iOS) and further observed that the issue disappears when the `TextField` does not span the full screen width; the root cause is likely the pointer leaving the hit-test area of the full-width field, ending the gesture. A test was skipped pending a fix. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-text-input.
summaries.122501: Comments confirmed the bug on web for both Android and iOS touch devices; the dismissal occurs specifically during fast or in-progress scrolls. A workaround was found: wrapping the form in `TextFieldTapRegion` prevents the unintended focus loss. Multiple commenters confirmed the workaround works. No upstream fix or PR linked; stalled with team-web.
summaries.153065: Comments confirmed the bug reproduces on stable 3.24.0 and master 3.24.0-pre with Japanese IME on macOS. The `oldText` field in a delta does not match the text produced by the previous delta when navigating the suggestion panel, indicating the engine sends inconsistent delta state. No fix or PR linked; stalled with team-text-input.
